name: Git Baseline
description: Protects protected branches from force-pushes and unreviewed direct pushes. Prevents accidental hard resets, dangerous untracked-file removal, and credential exposure in commits. Uses a tiered approach: outright block for force-push to main/master, require admin approval for normal pushes to protected branches, and require justification for other destructive git operations.

rules.name: Block force-push to main or master
rules.description: Force-pushing to main or master rewrites shared history and can permanently destroy commits. This is always blocked. Catches both flag orderings: git push -f origin main and git push origin main --force.

rules.name: Block detecting secrets in staged or committed content
rules.description: Scans pre-tool-use events (commit, add, diff) for API keys, tokens, and other credentials using the built-in gitleaks ruleset. Prevents accidental credential commits.

rules.name: Require access request to push to protected branches
rules.description: Direct pushes to main, master, production, or release branches bypass the pull-request review process. Requires admin approval.

rules.name: Require justification for force-push to any branch
rules.description: Force-pushing to any branch rewrites history for other contributors. Requires a written reason. (Force-push to main/master is blocked outright by a higher-priority rule.)

rules.name: Require justification for deleting remote branches
rules.description: git push --delete removes a remote branch, which may be in use by other contributors or CI pipelines.

rules.name: Require justification for git clean -f
rules.description: git clean -f permanently deletes untracked files with no recovery path. Requires a written reason.

rules.name: Warn on git reset --hard
rules.description: git reset --hard discards uncommitted changes. Logs a warning so developers are aware; does not block.
